user_query:
I need a quick, locally reproducible triage of whether Cloudflare's Rocket Loader is likely contributing to script order/deferral problems on a client page. I captured a single-page snapshot and console logs from staging and included a short Rocket Loader behavior note I assembled. Please do all analysis locally using the provided files under input/ and produce structured outputs under output/.

Inputs (already in the workspace):
- input/page_snapshot.html — the captured HTML of the page head/body with script tags and inline snippets.
- input/console_errors.jsonl — JSON Lines of browser console events with fields: timestamp, level, message, script_url. For inline scripts, script_url is in the form "inline:N" where N is the ordinal of inline scripts only, counted in HTML order starting at 1.
- input/script_catalog.csv — two columns: src, role. role is one of [payment, critical-library, analytics, chat, inline, other].
- input/rocketloader_doc.md — short notes on Rocket Loader behavior, incompatibilities, and mitigations.

What to deliver:
1) Write and run a script at scripts/analyze.py that accepts these exact args and produces the outputs below:
   - Required CLI: python3 scripts/analyze.py --html input/page_snapshot.html --errors input/console_errors.jsonl --catalog input/script_catalog.csv --doc input/rocketloader_doc.md --outdir output
   - The script must:
     a) Parse all <script> tags from input/page_snapshot.html in document order. For each script, extract: index (1-based by document order), src (or the literal "inline:N" if inline, where N is the inline-ordinal as defined above), all attributes as key=value pairs (preserve at least data-cfasync if present), has_data_cfasync (true/false), uses_document_write (true/false based on inline code), mentions_jquery (true/false based on inline code containing 'jQuery' or '$(').
     b) Aggregate input/console_errors.jsonl by script_url and by message. Compute error_count per script_url and the top error messages overall.
     c) Join the inventory from (a) with error counts from (b) and roles from input/script_catalog.csv by matching src (for inline entries, use the inline:N key). Derive deferred_by_rocketloader as (has_data_cfasync == false) based on the assumption in input/rocketloader_doc.md that data-cfasync="false" opts a script out of deferral.
     d) Output a CSV at output/scripts_with_errors.csv with columns: index, src, role, deferred_by_rocketloader (true/false), uses_document_write (true/false), mentions_jquery (true/false), error_count (integer). Include one row per script tag discovered.
     e) Rank scripts by error_count (descending), then by role severity with this tie-break order: payment > critical-library > inline > analytics > chat > other. Write the top 5 to output/top_issues.csv with the same columns as (d) plus a rank column (1..N).
     f) Write a JSON summary at output/rocketloader_impact.json with keys: total_scripts (int), count_deferred (int), count_opted_out (int), total_errors (int), top_error_messages (array of up to 3 objects with {"message": str, "count": int}, sorted by count desc).

2) Prepare a concise meeting note at output/meeting_notes.md that includes:
   - A title line "# Rocket Loader Triage Notes".
   - Executive summary: 3–5 sentences stating whether Rocket Loader likely contributes to the observed errors, explicitly referencing the "Incompatibilities" heading from input/rocketloader_doc.md.
   - Findings: a bulleted list for each entry in output/top_issues.csv, giving src, role, error_count, and a one-sentence rationale tying the issue to Rocket Loader behavior (e.g., order dependency, document.write), with one quote of the exact mitigation bullet text from the "How to Opt Out/Mitigations" section in input/rocketloader_doc.md for at least two of the items.
   - Action items: at least 4 concrete next steps with an owner (choose "web-dev" or "ops") and status ("To Do"). Include one item that instructs how to rerun the analysis with the exact command you used (the python3 command above).

Notes/assumptions to follow:
- Treat any script tag lacking data-cfasync="false" as deferred by Rocket Loader for the purpose of this analysis.
- Inline ordinal numbering for the inline:N keys must be determined from input/page_snapshot.html by counting only inline scripts in document order starting at 1.
- Do not fetch anything from the Internet; only use the provided inputs.

Deliverables to create under ./output:
- output/scripts_with_errors.csv
- output/top_issues.csv
- output/rocketloader_impact.json
- output/meeting_notes.md

I’ll review the CSVs/JSON for correctness and use the notes for a client call. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
